title: Honoring the life, achievements, legacy, and public service of former President James Earl "Jimmy" Carter, Jr.
plain_summary: This resolution honors former President Jimmy Carter who died on December 29, 2024. It celebrates his life and work from when he was born in Georgia in 1924 through his time as a Navy officer, governor, and president. The resolution also recognizes all the good work he did after being president, like starting the Carter Center to help people around the world and winning the Nobel Peace Prize. It says that President Carter made important contributions to Georgia, the United States, and the whole world, and that he should be remembered as one of America's great leaders.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
subjects: Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Resolution Text
sections.text: This resolution honors the life and service of former President James Earl Jimmy Carter Junior. Jimmy Carter was born on October first, nineteen twenty four in Plains, Georgia. His father was a peanut farmer and business owner, and his mother was a nurse who helped Black women with health care. When Jimmy was four years old, his family moved to Archery, Georgia, where they farmed crops like corn, peanuts, cotton, and sugarcane. Jimmy was the first person on his father's side of the family to graduate from high school. He studied engineering at Georgia Southwestern Junior College and then joined the Naval ROTC program at Georgia Tech. In nineteen forty three, he was accepted into the Naval Academy in Annapolis, Maryland. He graduated in nineteen forty six with a bachelor of science degree and served as a submarine officer in both the Atlantic and Pacific fleets, reaching the rank of lieutenant. Admiral Hyman Rickover chose him for the nuclear submarine program, and he studied reactor technology and nuclear physics at Union College in New York. He served as senior officer of the precommissioning crew of the Seawolf, which was the second nuclear submarine. Jimmy married Rosalynn Smith on July seventh, nineteen forty six, and they had four children named Jack, James the Third, Donnel, and Amy. In nineteen fifty three, after his father died, Jimmy left the Navy and returned to Plains, Georgia to run the family farms. He and Rosalynn worked hard to make the farming business successful again and became active in local politics. In nineteen fifty five, Jimmy won a seat on the Sumter County Board of Education and later became chairman. He was elected to the Georgia Senate in nineteen sixty two and was known as a practical politician who cut wasteful spending while supporting civil rights. Jimmy became Georgia's seventy sixth Governor on January twelfth, nineteen seventy one. As Governor, he publicly called for ending racial segregation and promoted education and prison reform while increasing the number of Black officials in state government. His biggest accomplishment as Governor was modernizing Georgia's government to make it more efficient. In nineteen seventy four, he served as campaign chairman for the Democratic National Committee. In nineteen seventy six, Jimmy became the Democratic Party's presidential nominee with running mate Walter Mondale from Minnesota. He was elected President on November second, nineteen seventy six. President Carter was known for connecting with the people he served and famously walked down Pennsylvania Avenue on his inauguration day with his wife and daughter. His foreign policy achievements included the Panama Canal treaties, the Camp David Accords which made peace between Egypt and Israel, the SALT Two treaty with the Soviet Union, and establishing diplomatic relations with China. On October seventeenth, nineteen seventy nine, President Carter signed a law creating the Department of Education. He encouraged Americans to save energy and use resources more efficiently. On August fourth, nineteen seventy seven, he signed a law creating the Department of Energy. In nineteen seventy eight, President Carter signed the National Energy Act which set goals to reduce dependence on oil, increase use of renewable energy like solar power, and require cars to get better gas mileage. In nineteen eighty, he signed the Energy Security Act which gave incentives for private companies to invest in renewable energy and included tax credits for businesses and homeowners. President Carter also protected wildlife by signing the Alaska National Interest Conservation Act of nineteen eighty to protect public lands and preserve Alaska Native culture and wilderness. After leaving the White House, President Carter became a professor at Emory University in Atlanta, Georgia, and founded the Carter Center with his wife Rosalynn. The Carter Center worked to resolve conflicts, promote democracy, protect human rights, and prevent disease, especially helping to nearly eliminate Guinea worm disease. President Carter and the Carter Center helped with peace efforts in many countries including Ethiopia, North Korea, Haiti, Sudan, Venezuela, and others. The Carter Center sent one hundred twenty five election observation missions to the Americas, Africa, and Asia. In nineteen eighty six, the Carter Presidential Center and Jimmy Carter Library and Museum were dedicated in his honor. In nineteen eighty seven, the Jimmy Carter National Historic Site was dedicated and was later designated as a national historic park in twenty twenty one. On December tenth, two thousand two, President Carter received the Nobel Peace Prize for his decades of work finding peaceful solutions to conflicts, advancing democracy and human rights, and promoting economic and social development. Jimmy and Rosalynn Carter volunteered one week every year for Habitat for Humanity to help people renovate and build homes in the United States and other countries until twenty twenty. Jimmy Carter was deeply committed to his faith and taught Sunday School at Maranatha Baptist Church in Plains, Georgia for more than forty years. When Rosalynn passed away, she and Jimmy had been married for seventy seven years and had four children, twelve grandchildren, and fourteen great grandchildren. On February eighteenth, twenty twenty three, President Carter decided to spend his remaining time at home with his family and receive hospice care. On December twenty ninth, twenty twenty four, President Carter passed away at home in Plains, Georgia. Jimmy Carter led his life and country with integrity, humility, and commitment to justice, championing human rights, prioritizing diplomacy, and fighting for marginalized communities.

sections.title: House Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives honors the life, achievements, legacy, and distinguished public service of James Jimmy Earl Carter Junior. The House is saddened by the death of James Earl Carter Junior and expresses its deepest condolences to his family. The House acknowledges the long lasting impacts of James Earl Carter Junior and his contributions to the State of Georgia, the United States, and the world. The House establishes the legacy of James Earl Carter Junior as one of the great leaders and statesmen of the United States.
